enter_plan_mode.description: switch into planning mode. while in plan mode, u won't have access 2 any tools — u can only output text. use this 2 produce a structured impl plan b4 executing changes. call exit_plan_mode when ready 2 resume tool exec. use when task is complex & benefits from upfront planning.

parameters.reason: why ur entering plan mode (shown 2 the user).

exit_plan_mode.description: exit planning mode & resume normal tool exec. call after u've produced ur plan & r ready 2 exec it w/ tools.

parameters.summary: brief summary of the plan ur about 2 exec.

enable_tools.description: REQUIRED after search_tools: activate tools discovered by search_tools so u can call them. u MUST call this after search_tools returns results where isEnabled is false — w/o calling enable_tools 1st, discovered tools CANNOT b used. accepts exact tool names (e.g. "get_weather") or domain prefixes (e.g. "domain:Finance", "domainKey:health") 2 activate an entire domain at once. newly enabled tools become avail on the NEXT iteration — u don't need 2 call them in the same turn.

parameters.tools: tool names or domain prefixes 2 enable. examples: ["get_weather", "get_weather_forecast"] or ["domain:Weather & Environment"].

disable_tools.description: dynamically disable tools from this convo 2 reduce token usage & tool interference. accepts exact tool names or domain prefixes (e.g. "domain:Finance"). core cognitive tools (memory, tasks, planning, orchestration) can't b disabled.

parameters.tools: tool names or domain prefixes 2 disable. examples: ["get_weather"] or ["domain:Weather & Environment"].

discover_and_enable_tools.description: search the FULL tool catalog ({{totalToolCount}} tools) AND auto-enable matches in 1 step. combines search_tools & enable_tools — after calling this, discovered tools r immediately avail on the next iteration w/o a separate enable_tools call. use when u know u want 2 use discovered tools right away. covers all domains: {{domainListLowercase}}. accepts same params as search_tools (query, domain, limit).

parameters.query: search keyword(s) 2 match against tool names & descs. use capability-specific terms 4 best results. examples: {{sampleKeywords}}.
parameters.domain: filter by tool domain. known domains: {{domainListQuoted}}.

create_skill.description: create a reusable workflow skill. skills r stored prompt templates w/ variable interpolation ({{variable}}) that can b invoked by name. use 2 capture multi-step workflows (refactor→test→commit, analyze→report, etc.) as reusable atomic ops. skills persist across sessions & can b shared across agents.

parameters.name: unique skill name (e.g. 'refactor_and_test', 'code_review'). used as the skill ID.
parameters.description: what the skill does — shown when listing skills.
parameters.prompt: the prompt template 2 exec. use {{variable}} syntax 4 params.
parameters.steps: optional: ordered list of step descs 4 documentation.
parameters.tools: optional: specific tools 2 enable. if omitted, all tools r avail.
parameters.maxIterations: optional: max agentic loop iterations 4 the skill run (1-100). default: 25.
parameters.model: optional: model override 4 the skill run.

execute_skill.description: exec a previously created skill by its ID. the skill's prompt template is interpolated w/ the provided vars & executed as an inline agentic task. use list_skills 2 see avail skills.

parameters.skillId: the skill ID 2 exec (derived from the skill name).
parameters.variables: key-value pairs 4 {{variable}} interpolation in the skill's prompt template.

list_skills.description: list all avail skills. skills r reusable workflow templates created w/ create_skill.

delete_skill.description: delete a skill by its ID.

enter_worktree.description: enter an isolated git worktree 4 the current convo. creates a new branch & redirects all file/git/shell tool calls 2 the worktree dir. use 4 risky refactors, experimental changes, or speculative edits w/o affecting the main branch. ur full convo ctx is preserved. call exit_worktree 2 merge or discard when done.

parameters.reason: why ur entering an isolated worktree (e.g. 'risky refactor', 'experimental approach').

exit_worktree.description: exit the current isolated worktree & return 2 the main workspace. choose 2 'merge' changes back 2 the main branch or 'discard' them entirely. if merging, changes r committed & merged auto.

parameters.action: 'merge' 2 apply changes 2 main branch, 'discard' 2 throw them away.
parameters.commitMessage: commit msg 4 the merge (used when action is 'merge'). auto-gen'd if not provided.

write_todo.description: write or update a persistent TODO checklist 4 the current proj. maintains a structured list of items w/ completion status. use 2 track multi-step work, record progress, & keep a living checklist that persists across convo turns. ea item has a status: 'pending', 'in_progress', or 'completed'. call w/ the full updated list — it replaces the prev state.

parameters.items: full list of todo items. replaces the prev list entirely.

summarize_conversation.description: produce a compressed summary of the current convo ctx. use when the convo is getting long & u need 2 consolidate ur understanding b4 continuing. the summary u write is stored & can b referenced in future turns 2 recover ctx. this is NOT shown 2 the user — it's ur private working memory.

parameters.summary: ur compressed summary of the convo so far. include: key decisions made, files modified, current task state, & what remains.
parameters.keyFiles: optional: list of key file paths relevant 2 the current work.

ask_user.description: ask the user 1 or more qs & wait 4 their responses b4 continuing. use when u need clarification, a decision between options, or explicit confirmation b4 proceeding w/ a potentially impactful action. the agent loop pauses until the user responds. u can batch up 2 4 related qs in a single call 2 reduce round-trips.

parameters.context: optional: additional ctx shown below the qs.
parameters.questions: batch of qs 2 ask the user (up 2 4). ea item is a question obj.

list_mcp_resources.description: list avail resources from a connected MCP server.

parameters.server_name: the MCP server name 2 query. if omitted, queries all.

read_mcp_resource.description: read a specific resource from a connected MCP server by its URI.

parameters.server_name: the MCP server name that hosts the resource.

authenticate_mcp_server.description: authenticate w/ a connected MCP server by providing credentials.

parameters.server_name: the MCP server name 2 authenticate w/.

set_timer.description: set a short 1-shot timer 2 pause, wait, or defer exec within this convo. use when u need 2 wait 4 an async process 2 finish, poll a build or deployment, retry after a transient failure, or yield briefly b4 continuing work. the timer fires the provided prompt back into this convo after the specified duration. duration must b between 30 & 599 seconds (under 10 min). this is an agent-internal tool 4 in-convo waits only — do NOT use 4 user-facing reminders, alarms, or scheduled events (use create_cron_job instead).

parameters.prompt: the instruction or ctx 2 inject back into this convo when the timer fires.
parameters.durationSeconds: # of seconds 2 wait b4 firing (30–599). must b under 10 min.

list_timers.description: list all active 1-shot agent timers in the current convo. shows short-duration waits (30–599 seconds) set by set_timer. 2 view user-facing scheduled reminders, alarms, & recurring events, use list_cron_jobs instead.

cancel_timer.description: cancel an active 1-shot agent timer in the current convo. 2 delete a scheduled cron job, reminder, or alarm, use delete_cron_job instead.

parameters.timerId: the unique ID of the timer 2 cancel.

search_conversations.description: search past agent convos using a natural lang query. finds prev sessions by semantic similarity 2 the query text. returns convo titles, summaries, dates, & linked memory counts. use when the user references a past session, asks 'remember when we...', or when u need ctx from a prev coding session.

parameters.query: natural lang search query describing what ur looking 4. example: 'MCP connection debugging', 'the session where we fixed WebSocket drops'.
